c.d: The ability of the app to sync notes and be used across multiple devices and operating systems such as iOS, Android, Windows, macOS, and web browsers
Supports handwriting input, conversion of handwritten notes to text, and optimized stylus integration for devices like Apple Pencil or Surface Pen
Includes ability to organize notes using tags, folders, sections, subsections, and hierarchical structures for easy retrieval and management
Allows multiple users to share, collaborate in real-time or asynchronously on notes, lists, and projects
Support for inserting images, sketches, tables, PDFs, audio, video, and other media within notes
Ability to write notes in Markdown, rich text, or other markup languages for better formatting and readability
Cost structure such as free, freemium, or paid plans with various feature tiers
User reviews and ratings on platforms such as G2, Capterra, and app stores indicating app reliability and user satisfaction
